_notes: Australia renewable energy market data. Last enriched with live research March 2026.

marketOverview.currentRenewableShare: ~40% of electricity generation (Q4 2025), rising rapidly; renewables+storage hit 51% of NEM supply in Dec 2025 quarter
marketOverview.installedCapacityFY2425: Record 4.4 GW new generation and storage commissioned FY2024-25
marketOverview.q42025Commissioning: 1.8 GW commissioned in Q4 2025 alone (2 solar + 7 BESS projects)
marketOverview.gridConnectionPipeline: Record 64 GW in grid connection process (NEM, Q4 2025) - up 14% quarter-on-quarter; BESS claimed 46% share
marketOverview.rooftopSolar: 25.1 GW total rooftop solar in NEM (Nov 2025). World's highest per-capita uptake. AEMO projects 53 GW by 2030.
marketOverview.totalCapexNeeded2030: ~A$73B+ investment expected under CIS alone. AEMO ISP estimates A$120B+ total to 2030.
marketOverview.aemoLongTermForecast: Total NEM generation/storage to triple from 92 GW today to 297 GW by 2050. Grid-scale wind+solar to reach 58 GW by 2030.
marketOverview.wholesalePrices: NEM wholesale electricity averaged AU$50/MWh in Dec 2025 quarter — nearly halved vs prior year, driven by record renewables output.
keyTechnologies: Battery Storage (BESS) — dominant Q4 2025 activity, 46% of pipeline

capacityInvestmentScheme.name: Capacity Investment Scheme (CIS)
capacityInvestmentScheme.description: Federal government scheme underwriting new firm and dispatchable capacity. Provides revenue support for new renewable and storage projects.
capacityInvestmentScheme.target: 40 GW of new capacity nationally by 2030 (uplifted July 2025 from original 32 GW target)
capacityInvestmentScheme.mechanism: Contracts for Difference (CfDs) - government tops up or claws back revenue based on market prices
capacityInvestmentScheme.expectedInvestment: ~A$73B in electricity sector investment supported by CIS

tenderProgress.tender3: Awarded Sep 2025 - 4.13 GW / 15.37 GWh battery storage (16 projects, largest battery tender to date globally)
tenderProgress.tender4: Awarded mid-2025 - 6.6 GW renewable generation (20 projects, 12 are hybrid with battery)
tenderProgress.tender5_6: WEM tenders closed Nov 2025 - 1.6 GW generation + 2,400 MWh storage, results expected Mar-Apr 2026
tenderProgress.tender7: NEM generation - bidding closed Dec 2025, 5 GW target, results expected May 2026
tenderProgress.tender8: NEM storage - open, seeking 16 GWh (largest single CIS storage procurement), results mid-2026
tenderProgress.cumulativeAwarded: 13 GW generation + 5.78 GW / 21.57 GWh dispatchable capacity awarded to date

capacityInvestmentScheme.keyRisk: Only ~28% of battery capacity awarded a CISA has reached FID - zombie project risk for lowest-cost bids unable to secure debt financing

keyPolicy.arena: Australian Renewable Energy Agency - grants and concessional finance for RE innovation
keyPolicy.cleanEnergyFinanceCorp: CEFC provides A$10B+ in low-cost finance for clean energy projects

accccMergerRegime.description: New ACCC mandatory merger control regime in effect from 1 January 2026
accccMergerRegime.impact: ACCC approval now required before completing transactions crossing certain thresholds. More rigorous competitive impact assessment especially for battery storage, grid infrastructure, and cross-border transactions.

stateSchemes.queensland: Queensland Energy and Jobs Plan - 70% renewables by 2032, 80% by 2035
stateSchemes.nsw: Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap - 12 GW renewable by 2030
stateSchemes.westernAustralia: Wholesale electricity market reform underway; CIS Tenders 5 & 6 targeting WEM

firb.description: Foreign Investment Review Board - reviews foreign investment in sensitive sectors
firb.threshold: A$0 threshold for foreign government investors in electricity generation
firb.privateThreshold: A$330M for private foreign investors in electricity generation (critical infrastructure)
firb.recentTrend: Increased scrutiny on Chinese-linked investors. FIRB rejected several Chinese RE deals 2022-2024.
firb.keyRisk: Chinese SOE-linked bidders face near-certain FIRB rejection for Australian RE assets
firb.sembcorpNote: Sembcorp/Alinta deal (announced Dec 2025) awaiting FIRB approval - Singapore-linked expected to pass. Key watchpoint H1 2026.

bessMarket.overview: Australia is the world's third-largest utility-scale BESS market (behind China and US, surpassing UK in Oct 2025), and the first nation to surpass 1 GWh of utility battery capacity per million people. BESS dominated Q4 2025 investment with 16 deals; renewables+storage delivered 51% of NEM supply in the Dec 2025 quarter.
bessMarket.globalRanking: 3rd largest utility BESS market globally (as of Oct 2025, surpassed UK)
bessMarket.perCapitaLeader: World's first nation to exceed 1 GWh of utility battery capacity per million people
bessMarket.deployment2025: Record 11 projects / 1.9 GW / 4.9 GWh commissioned in 2025 — surpassing the entire 2017–2024 combined BESS output for Australia

nemCapacity.endOf2025: ~4.6 GW operational BESS (4.8 GW / 12 GWh added in 2025 alone)
nemCapacity.aemo2050Target: 40 GW of battery storage required by 2050 — a 433% increase from today's ~7.5 GW fleet

pipeline.by2027Forecast: Up to 16.8 GW operational by end 2027; ~12.5 GW expected by 2027
pipeline.cisAward: 5.78 GW / 21.57 GWh awarded under CIS dispatchable tenders (as of Mar 2026)
pipeline.gridShare: Battery storage claimed 46% share of Australia's record 64 GW grid connection pipeline (Q4 2025)
pipeline.durationTrend: Average duration shifted from 2.0 hrs (2017) to 2.6 hrs (2025). 4-hour systems dominate from 2027 onwards.

investment.q12025: A$2.4B invested in large-scale BESS in Q1 2025 — 83% above recent yearly averages; second-highest quarterly investment ever
investment.q42025Deals: 16 BESS deals in Q4 2025 (acquisitions, financings, PPAs); NSW led with >1 GW of activity
investment.totalInvested2025: ~A$8-10B estimated total BESS investment committed in 2025

capexBenchmarks.2024: A$700–1,100/kWh (falling rapidly; LFP cell costs declining)
capexBenchmarks.2025 (CSIRO GenCost 2025): 11–16% cost reduction vs 2024; global avg US$117/kWh (~A$185/kWh) turnkey (BloombergNEF, 31% decline from 2024)
capexBenchmarks.2026 outlook: Further 10–15% expected decline as LFP supply chain normalises; 4-hr systems approaching A$600–800/kWh

keyProjects.name: Waratah Super Battery
keyProjects.status: Stage 1 (350 MW/700 MWh) energised and registered in NEM Aug 2025; world's largest single-site BESS. Full project online expected 2026.
keyProjects.notes: Largest, fastest and most flexible BESS in the world. 288 Hitachi inverters, 3,598 battery units (Rept Battero + EVE Energy). Connecting to Transgrid network.

keyProjects.name: Hornsdale Power Reserve
keyProjects.status: Operational 2017; world's first large-scale commercial BESS. Expanded 2020. Provides SIPS grid services.
keyProjects.notes: Pioneer project that proved commercial viability of grid-scale BESS in Australia. Owned via Neoen (acquired by Brookfield Apr 2025).

keyProjects.name: Victorian Big Battery (VBB)
keyProjects.status: Operational 2021, Moorabool, VIC. Part of A$950M Neoen Victoria portfolio acquired by CPP/Brookfield Aug 2025.

keyProjects.name: Hanworth BESS
keyProjects.status: Development — largest planned BESS project in Australia

keyProjects.name: Origin Eraring BESS
keyProjects.status: Commissioned late 2025 at the retiring Eraring coal site; coal-to-BESS repurposing model

keyProjects.name: Wooreen BESS
keyProjects.status: Financially committed Q1 2025; largest single project secured in Q1 2025

keyProjects.name: Western Downs BESS Stage 2
keyProjects.status: Commissioned Sep 2025, six weeks ahead of schedule

pipelineByState.NSW: Largest share of Q4 2025 BESS deal activity (>1 GW). Waratah Super Battery, Eraring BESS, Hanworth BESS (1.2 GW), multiple CIS Tender 3 winners (5 NSW projects).
pipelineByState.VIC: Victorian Big Battery, Wooreen BESS, Elaine BESS, Golden Plains BESS. VRET 95% by 2035 driving deployment. CIS Tender 3 awarded 5 VIC projects.
pipelineByState.QLD: Western Downs BESS, Punchs Creek BESS (JV). CIS Tender 3 awarded 4 QLD battery projects. Queensland Energy and Jobs Plan 80% by 2035.
pipelineByState.SA: 640 MW / 1.8 GWh committed in Q1 2025. Hornsdale Power Reserve. Already >70% renewable penetration — SA leads NEM.
pipelineByState.WA: WEM reform underway; CIS Tenders 5 & 6 targeting 2,400 MWh WEM storage. Results expected Mar-Apr 2026. Slower BESS market vs NEM.

keyDevelopers.name: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock/GIP)
keyDevelopers.pipeline: 4 GWh under construction, 13 GWh development pipeline including Waratah Super Battery. Major CIS Tender 3 contract holder.

keyDevelopers.name: Neoen (Brookfield)
keyDevelopers.pipeline: Hornsdale Power Reserve, Victorian Big Battery, Western Downs BESS. Key CIS contract holder. Neoen portfolio acquired by Brookfield Apr 2025.

keyDevelopers.name: Origin Energy
keyDevelopers.pipeline: Eraring BESS (460 MW / 1.84 GWh, operational). Additional BESS pipeline at retiring coal sites.

keyDevelopers.name: Octopus Energy Australia (APG-backed)
keyDevelopers.pipeline: Hanworth BESS (1.2 GW / 4.8 GWh — largest planned AU BESS). USD 650M APG capital committed Jul 2025.

keyDevelopers.name: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners
keyDevelopers.pipeline: Supernode BESS and wind portfolio. 5 GW development pipeline. Raised A$400M Aug 2025.

keyDevelopers.name: Pacific Green Technologies
keyDevelopers.pipeline: 7 GWh BESS portfolio (for sale as part of Australian retreat in 2025).

m_and_a_trends.q42025Activity: 16 BESS deals in Q4 2025 — acquisitions, project financings, and PPAs. NEM added 4.8 GW / 12 GWh in 2025.
m_and_a_trends.solarLonger_viable_standalone: Solar projects without integrated BESS increasingly unfinanceable due to negative daytime pricing. Hybrid solar+BESS now dominant for M&A/financing.
m_and_a_trends.durationShift: Average battery duration shifting to 4-hour systems from 2027 — higher value for M&A.
m_and_a_trends.zombieRisk: Only ~28% FID rate on CIS-awarded battery projects — creating advisory opportunities around distressed/undercapitalised developers.
m_and_a_trends.coalSiteOpportunity: 11 GW of coal closure over next decade creates BESS-at-coal-site M&A theme (Eraring, Yallourn, Callide B).
m_and_a_trends.globalRankingDeal: BESS M&A deal value increased ~57% globally from 2024 to 2025; trend expected to continue into 2026.

bessMarket.csCapitalContext: CS Capital specialises in BESS M&A advisory — buy-side and sell-side mandates. Key focus areas: CIS-contracted projects approaching FID, DA-approved or construction-ready BESS assets, hybrid solar+BESS platforms, and coal-site BESS repurposing (Eraring model). The ~28% FID rate on CIS-awarded projects creates advisory opportunities around undercapitalised developers seeking strategic acquirers.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: Brookfield Renewable Holdings (with Temasek) completed full acquisition of Neoen. Initial 53.12% stake acquired Dec 30, 2024 (EUR 3.25B). Tender offer price EUR 39.85/share. Neoen delisted April 4, 2025 after mandatory squeeze-out. Neoen operates ~10 GW target capacity globally with major Australian assets incl. Hornsdale Power Reserve.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Landmark transaction — among the largest renewable energy M&A deals globally. Validates institutional appetite for large-scale Australian RE platforms. Neoen assets include Hornsdale Power Reserve and Victorian Big Battery.

majorTransactions2024_2026.date: Announced December 11, 2025. Shareholder EGM approved Jan 30, 2026. Completion expected H1 2026.
majorTransactions2024_2026.description: Sembcorp Industries (SGX-listed Singapore utility) agreed to acquire 100% of Alinta Energy from Chow Tai Fook Enterprises. Alinta has 3,400 MW owned/contracted generation (gas, wind, solar, coal) across AU/NZ plus 10.4 GW pipeline of renewables and firming. Deal is immediately EPS accretive (+9%). Funded fully in cash via bridge facility.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Major entry of Asian strategic into Australian power market. Supports Sembcorp's target of 25 GW renewables capacity by 2028. Awaiting FIRB and ACCC approvals. ACCC approval now required under new Jan 2026 merger control regime.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: CPP Investments (Canada Pension Plan) acquired the Neoen Victoria portfolio for A$950M, comprising 652 MW including the Victorian Big Battery (300 MW/450 MWh), Bulgana Wind Farm, and Numurkah Solar Farm.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Validates premium pricing for operating BESS assets in NEM. Victorian Big Battery is now CPP-owned within the Brookfield/Neoen structure.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: La Caisse de depot et placement du Quebec (CDPQ, rebranded 'La Caisse') agreed to acquire Edify Energy, a major Australian solar and battery storage developer. Edify has 11 GW+ pipeline.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Major Canadian pension capital deployment. Edify is a top-tier Australian BESS developer with significant CIS contracts. Rebranding reflects CDPQ's global infrastructure ambitions.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: Dutch pension giant APG committed USD 650M to Octopus Energy's Australian platform. Octopus subsequently acquired the 1.2 GW/4.8 GWh Hanworth BESS project — Australia's largest planned battery storage project.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Major European pension capital deployment into Australian BESS. Octopus is now a top-tier Australian RE and BESS investor/developer.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: BlackRock Infrastructure (via GIP acquisition) took a stake in AGL's renewable development pipeline.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Global fund managers deepening Australian RE exposure. BlackRock also holds Akaysha Energy (1 GW+ BESS under construction, 13 GWh pipeline).

majorTransactions2024_2026.deal: Macquarie GIG / multiple wind, solar and battery assets
majorTransactions2024_2026.description: Macquarie Asset Management (A$941B global AUM, tops IPE Real Assets 100) continued active acquisition and development via GIG and new Aula Energy platform (4 GW wind/solar/battery pipeline AU/NZ).
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Macquarie remains most active infrastructure investor in Australian RE.

majorTransactions2024_2026.description: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners raised A$400M for continued Australian wind and BESS investment. Portfolio now 1.5 GW+ operational/construction, 5 GW development pipeline.
majorTransactions2024_2026.significance: Australia-focused specialist RE manager expanding BESS and wind development pipeline.

keyTransmission.description: 500 kV transmission line NSW - connects Snowy 2.0 to Sydney/Canberra load centers
New interconnector Victoria-NSW, enables more renewable transfer
Undersea cable Tasmania-Victoria, unlocks Tasmanian hydro battery
2 GW pumped hydro expansion of Snowy Scheme
keyTransmission.capex: A$12B+ (heavily over budget vs original A$2B)

MacquarieGIG.aum: A$941B global AUM (tops IPE Real Assets Top 100 Infrastructure Managers 2025)
MacquarieGIG.australiaActivity: Most active RE investor/developer in Australia. Launched Aula Energy (4 GW AU/NZ pipeline).

Brookfield.australiaActivity: Completed Neoen acquisition (EUR 6.1B / ~A$10B) April 2025. Major Australian RE presence including Hornsdale and Victorian Big Battery.

BlackRock.aum: USD 10T+ total AUM, USD 170B+ infrastructure (incl. GIP acquired 2024)
BlackRock.australiaActivity: Growing Australian RE exposure via GIP. Controls Akaysha Energy (4 GWh under construction, 13 GWh BESS pipeline).

IFM.australiaActivity: Significant infrastructure investor, growing RE allocation

AustralianSuper.australiaActivity: Increasing direct RE investment, targets 10%+ infrastructure allocation

QIC.australiaActivity: A$12B in RE assets (2024). Plans A$15B+ in energy transition over next 5 years. Active in QLD via PowAR consortium.
QIC.recentCapex: A$12B RE assets; A$15B+ 5-year investment plan

CBUS.australiaActivity: Growing infrastructure/RE allocation via direct and fund investments

Quinbrook.australiaActivity: Australia-focused RE developer/investor. Raised A$400M Aug 2025. 1.5 GW+ operational portfolio, 5 GW pipeline.

Tepco.australiaActivity: Acquired 5GW+ renewable pipeline assets in Australia

Amp.australiaActivity: Active in Australian RE development and investment

Octopus.australiaActivity: Received USD 650M from APG (Jul 2025). Acquired Hanworth BESS (1.2 GW/4.8 GWh). Rapidly expanding.
Octopus.recentCapex: USD 650M raise from APG, deploying in AU BESS

Sembcorp.australiaActivity: Agreed to acquire Alinta Energy (A$6.5B EV). Completion expected H1 2026 pending FIRB/ACCC. Will have 3.4 GW + 10.4 GW pipeline.

recentNews.headline: Battery deal surge dominates Australian RE market
recentNews.detail: Investor activity in Australian energy storage intensifying with multiple transactions — purchases, sales, financing arrangements, and PPAs for big batteries. BESS dominated investment in Q4 2025 with 16 deals totalling 2.16 GW.

recentNews.headline: Victoria's second-largest battery connects to grid
recentNews.detail: A major BESS facility adjacent to Victoria's largest gas generation infrastructure connected to the grid management system, accelerating BESS deployment across NEM. Household battery rebate demand also hit record levels.

recentNews.headline: NSW Planning Minister advances three wind projects
recentNews.detail: NSW Planning Minister resolved legal challenges blocking three wind energy development applications, advancing projects toward approval. Signals continued government support for wind development in NSW.

recentNews.headline: Fortescue begins WA's largest solar facility construction
recentNews.detail: Fortescue commenced construction of Western Australia's largest solar facility as part of its Pilbara decarbonisation ('real zero') initiative.

recentNews.headline: Spanish RE company completes Australian wind farm acquisition
recentNews.detail: A Spanish renewable energy company completed purchase of a formerly record-holding Australian wind farm, with plans to alleviate grid constraints through transmission upgrades.

recentNews.headline: Sembcorp shareholders approve A$6.5B Alinta Energy acquisition
recentNews.detail: Sembcorp Industries received shareholder EGM approval (Jan 30, 2026) for the A$6.5B Alinta Energy deal. Completion expected H1 2026 subject to FIRB and ACCC regulatory approvals. Alinta has 10.4 GW renewables pipeline.

recentNews.headline: ACCC mandatory merger control regime takes effect
recentNews.detail: New mandatory merger clearance regime in force from 1 January 2026. All major M&A transactions in energy and infrastructure sectors must seek ACCC approval before completing. Adds new regulatory hurdle to Australian RE deals.

recentNews.headline: CIS Tender 3 awards 4.13 GW / 15.37 GWh battery storage — largest battery tender globally
recentNews.detail: Australia's CIS third tender awarded contracts to 16 battery projects across NEM (5 NSW, 5 VIC, 4 QLD, 2 SA). Cumulative CIS awards now 13 GW generation + 5.78 GW/21.57 GWh dispatchable capacity. Government also uplifted CIS target to 40 GW in July 2025.

recentNews.headline: Waratah Super Battery Stage 1 energised — world's largest BESS
recentNews.detail: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock/GIP) energised the first 350 MW/700 MWh stage of the Waratah Super Battery in NSW, registering it in the NEM. Full 850 MW/1.6 GWh project on track. The facility features 3,598 battery units and 288 Hitachi inverters.

recentNews.headline: CPP Investments acquires Neoen Victoria portfolio for A$950M
recentNews.detail: CPP Investments acquired the Neoen Victoria portfolio for A$950M, comprising 652 MW including Victorian Big Battery (300 MW/450 MWh), Bulgana Wind Farm, and Numurkah Solar Farm.

recentNews.headline: APG commits USD 650M to Octopus Energy Australia
recentNews.detail: Dutch pension giant APG invested USD 650M in Octopus Energy's Australian platform. Octopus subsequently acquired the 1.2 GW/4.8 GWh Hanworth BESS — Australia's largest planned battery project.

recentNews.headline: Brookfield completes EUR 6.1B Neoen acquisition, delists from Euronext Paris
recentNews.detail: Brookfield Renewable Holdings (with Temasek) completed full acquisition after mandatory squeeze-out on April 4, 2025. Neoen delisted from Euronext Paris. Neoen assets include Hornsdale Power Reserve and Victorian Big Battery.

recentNews.headline: Sembcorp announces A$6.5B Alinta Energy acquisition
recentNews.detail: Singapore's Sembcorp Industries signed share sale agreement to acquire 100% of Alinta Energy from Chow Tai Fook Enterprises for A$6.5B EV (A$5.6B purchase price). Supports Sembcorp's target of 25 GW renewables by 2028. Deal not yet completed — awaiting FIRB/ACCC.

recentNews.headline: Australia Q4 2025: Record NEM capacity additions, BESS dominates
recentNews.detail: Q4 2025 was a record quarter with 1.8 GW commissioned (NEM), 7 of which were BESS projects. Full FY2024-25 saw record 4.4 GW. Grid connection pipeline hit record 64 GW (up 14%), with BESS claiming 46% share. Renewables+storage reached 51% of NEM supply in Dec quarter.

recentNews.headline: Australia becomes world's 3rd-largest utility BESS market, surpassing UK
recentNews.detail: Australia surpassed the UK to become the third-largest market for utility-scale BESS globally. Now first nation to exceed 1 GWh of utility battery capacity per million people. 2025 saw 4.9 GWh commissioned — more than the 2017-2024 combined total.

recentNews.headline: FIRB continues to scrutinise Chinese-linked RE investments
recentNews.detail: FIRB rejected or deterred multiple bids from Chinese state-owned or linked entities for Australian RE assets on national security grounds. Zero-dollar threshold applies to all foreign government investors.

domesticPlayers.name: AGL Energy
domesticPlayers.description: Australia's largest electricity generator (coal + gas + renewables). Undergoing coal exit with major BESS investments at retiring coal plant sites. Part of PowAR consortium (with QIC and Future Fund). Owns Bayswater and Loy Yang A coal plants retiring 2030-35.
domesticPlayers.focus: Utility-scale wind, solar, BESS; thermal transition

domesticPlayers.name: Origin Energy
domesticPlayers.description: Major NEM generator and gas retailer. Owns Eraring coal plant (NSW, closing 2025). Significant renewables development pipeline. 49% stake in Australia Pacific LNG. Building large BESS at Eraring site post-closure.
domesticPlayers.focus: Large-scale BESS, offshore wind, solar farms

domesticPlayers.name: Macquarie Asset Management / Green Investment Group (GIG)
domesticPlayers.aum_or_assets: A$941B global AUM (IPE Real Assets #1 infrastructure manager 2025); 5+ GW in Australia
domesticPlayers.description: World's largest infrastructure investor. Dominant in Australian RE through GIG platform. Also launched Aula Energy (4 GW AU/NZ wind/solar/battery pipeline). Manages Potentia Energy acquisition, QIC Solar JV, and numerous assets.
domesticPlayers.focus: Wind, solar, BESS; platform acquisitions; global capital deployment

domesticPlayers.name: IFM Investors
domesticPlayers.description: Owned by 17 Australian industry super funds. Significant Australian infrastructure investor (airports, ports, utilities). Increasing RE allocation via direct investments and fund mandates.
domesticPlayers.focus: Large-scale infrastructure platforms; patient long-duration capital

domesticPlayers.name: QIC (Queensland Investment Corporation)
domesticPlayers.aum_or_assets: A$100B+ AUM; A$12B in RE assets (2024)
domesticPlayers.description: Queensland state government investment manager. Active RE investor via PowAR consortium with AGL and Future Fund. QIC Infrastructure managing Punchs Creek solar+BESS JV (480MWp + 1.6GWh, QLD). Plans A$15B+ in energy transition over next 5 years.
domesticPlayers.focus: Solar + BESS; Queensland focus; energy transition platform

domesticPlayers.name: AustralianSuper
domesticPlayers.description: Australia's largest super fund. Direct infrastructure investor increasingly active in RE. Holds stakes in multiple wind and solar farms. Targets 10%+ infrastructure allocation. Growing direct RE mandate.

domesticPlayers.name: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock / GIP)
domesticPlayers.aum_or_assets: 4 GWh under construction, 13 GWh development pipeline
domesticPlayers.description: Australia's leading large-scale BESS developer. Acquired by BlackRock/GIP. Developed the Waratah Super Battery (world's largest). Key beneficiary of CIS battery tenders.

domesticPlayers.name: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners
domesticPlayers.aum_or_assets: USD 7B+ committed capital; 1.5 GW+ AU portfolio
domesticPlayers.description: Australia-focused RE developer and investor. Raised A$400M in Aug 2025. Portfolio 1.5GW+ operational/construction, 5GW development pipeline. Known for Supernode BESS and wind projects.
domesticPlayers.focus: Wind, BESS, grid-scale solar; development and acquisition

domesticPlayers.name: Future Fund
domesticPlayers.description: Federal government wealth fund. Infrastructure allocation includes RE assets. Member of PowAR consortium with AGL and QIC. Generally passive, long-duration investor.

australiaRenewableTargets.totalNewCapacityNeeded: Grid-scale wind+solar from 23 GW to 58 GW by 2030 (AEMO ISP)
australiaRenewableTargets.annualInvestmentNeeded: 5.2-10.1 GW of new capacity annually over next 5 years
australiaRenewableTargets.storagePipeline: 5.78 GW / 21.57 GWh awarded under CIS dispatchable tenders to date
australiaRenewableTargets.bessTarget2050: 40 GW battery storage required in NEM by 2050 (AEMO)
australiaRenewableTargets.aemo2050Target: Total NEM capacity to triple from 92 GW to 297 GW; wind+solar to reach 120 GW
